title: Creating and Managing Views
description: Demonstrates CREATE VIEW, updating through view, and DROP VIEW operations
code:
-- Simple View (Updatable)
CREATE VIEW emp_details AS
SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, salary
FROM employees
WHERE department_id = 10;

-- View data
SELECT * FROM emp_details;

-- Update through view (works for simple views)
UPDATE emp_details SET salary = 5500 WHERE employee_id = 100;

-- Complex View (Not directly updatable)
CREATE VIEW dept_salary_summary AS
SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS emp_count, 
 AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
FROM employees
GROUP BY department_id;

-- Drop view
DROP VIEW emp_details;
explanation: Simple views based on single tables without aggregates can be updated directly. Complex views with GROUP BY, aggregates, or joins cannot be updated directly.

complexity.space: O(1) for regular view, O(n) for materialized view

title: Trigger Implementation
description: Shows creation of BEFORE INSERT and AFTER UPDATE triggers for audit logging
code:
-- Create audit table
CREATE TABLE employee_audit (
 audit_id NUMBER PRIMARY KEY,
 employee_id NUMBER,
 action VARCHAR2(50),
 old_salary NUMBER,
 new_salary NUMBER,
 change_date DATE
);

-- BEFORE INSERT Trigger
CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER trg_before_insert
BEFORE INSERT ON employees
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
 :NEW.hire_date := SYSDATE;
 :NEW.created_by := USER;
END;

-- AFTER UPDATE Trigger
CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER trg_after_update
AFTER UPDATE OF salary ON employees
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
 INSERT INTO employee_audit 
 VALUES (emp_audit_seq.NEXTVAL, :OLD.employee_id, 
 'SALARY UPDATE', :OLD.salary, :NEW.salary, SYSDATE);
END;
explanation: BEFORE triggers modify data before insertion. AFTER triggers perform actions after DML operations, commonly used for audit trails.

title: Sequence and Synonym Usage
description: Demonstrates creating and using sequences for auto-generation and synonyms for object aliasing
explanation: Sequences generate unique numeric values. Synonyms provide alternative names for tables, views, sequences making queries simpler.

title: Transaction Control Commands
description: Demonstrates transaction management with COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and SAVEPOINT
explanation: SAVEPOINT allows partial rollback within a transaction. COMMIT makes changes permanent. ROLLBACK undoes all uncommitted changes.

complexity.time: O(n) where n is number of affected rows

title: GRANT and REVOKE Privileges
description: Shows how to grant and revoke object and system privileges to database users
explanation: GRANT gives privileges, REVOKE removes them. WITH GRANT OPTION allows the grantee to grant the same privilege to others.
